title: COMMCARD PROGRAM - 211info
text: ARC OF BENTON COUNTY COMMCARD PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a program meant to facilitate understanding and effective communication techniques between an individual with a developmental disability and another person. Assistance is given to acquire an identification card with information about a person's diagnosis, address, advocate contacts, behavioral presentations, communication accomodations, and a sample stress-diffusing topic. Applicants will be required to attend a training session to practice the process with uniformed helping professionals. 928 NW Beca Avenue Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Adults with disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (541) 753-1711 3 . Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Application (541) 753-1711 ext 3 Misha [email protected] Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call, email, or visit website for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake AREA SERVED: Benton County SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: West off 9th Street onto Beca Avenue. Store on corner of 10th Street and Beca Avenue. Route 2 of City Transit System stops on 9th Street near Beca Avenue. Services: Identification Cards * Disabilities Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: MATERIAL GOODS - ARC THRIFT STORES SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT AND ENRICHMENT - ADVOCACY AND DAY SUPPORTS ACTIVITIES If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. ARC OF BENTON COUNTY
